text: I have fought against white domination, and I have fought against black domination. I have cherished the ideal of a democratic and free society in which all persons live together in harmony and with equal opportunities.
speech: Statement from the Dock at the Rivonia Trial
context: The closing of his famous speech from the dock; he expected to be sentenced to death.

text: It is an ideal which I hope to live for and to achieve. But if needs be, it is an ideal for which I am prepared to die.
context: The most quoted line of his Rivonia Trial address.

text: During my lifetime I have dedicated myself to this struggle of the African people. I have fought against white domination, and I have fought against black domination.
context: Establishing his lifelong commitment to non-racial democracy.

text: I have cherished the ideal of a democratic and free society in which all persons live together in harmony and with equal opportunities. It is an ideal which I hope to live for and to achieve.
context: Vision of the South Africa he wanted to help create.

text: Education is the most powerful weapon which you can use to change the world.
source: Speech at the University of the Witwatersrand
context: Often quoted; delivered in various forms throughout his post-presidency.

text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
context: Signature expression of perseverance drawn from the anti-apartheid struggle.

text: For to be free is not merely to cast off one's chains, but to live in a way that respects and enhances the freedom of others.
source: Long Walk to Freedom (autobiography; often delivered in speeches)
context: Defining freedom as relational and responsible, not merely individual.

text: No one is born hating another person because of the color of his skin, or his background, or his religion. People must learn to hate, and if they can learn to hate, they can be taught to love.
context: Core belief in the possibility of moral transformation and reconciliation.

text: I learned that courage was not the absence of fear, but the triumph over it. The brave man is not he who does not feel afraid, but he who conquers that fear.
context: Personal definition of courage forged in decades of imprisonment.

text: If you want to make peace with your enemy, you have to work with your enemy. Then he becomes your partner.
source: Long Walk to Freedom / post-presidency interviews
context: The philosophy behind the Truth and Reconciliation Commission.

text: There is no passion to be found playing small — in settling for a life that is less than the one you are capable of living.
context: Encouraging people to live up to their full potential.

text: As I walked out the door toward the gate that would lead to my freedom, I knew if I didn't leave my bitterness and hatred behind, I'd still be in prison.
context: On the personal decision to pursue reconciliation rather than revenge upon release.

text: A good head and a good heart are always a formidable combination.
context: Valuing both intellect and compassion in leadership.

text: I am fundamentally an optimist. Whether that comes from nature or nurture, I cannot say. Part of being optimistic is keeping one's head pointed toward the sun, one's feet moving forward.
context: Personal philosophy that sustained him through 27 years in prison.

text: Freedom is indivisible; the chains on any one of my people were the chains on all of them, the chains on all of my people were the chains on me.
context: Solidarity as the foundation of the struggle.

text: We dedicate this day to all the heroes and heroines in this country and the rest of the world who sacrificed in many ways and surrendered their lives so that we could be free.
context: First speech as President of a democratic South Africa.

text: Out of the experience of an extraordinary human disaster that lasted too long, must be born a society of which all humanity will be proud.
context: Transforming the pain of apartheid into a gift to the world.

text: Let freedom reign. The sun never set on so glorious a human achievement.
context: Celebrating the first non-racial democratic election.

text: There is nothing like returning to a place that remains unchanged to find the ways in which you yourself have altered.
context: On revisiting Robben Island and other sites after release.

text: Real leaders must be ready to sacrifice all for the freedom of their people.
source: Rivonia Trial Statement / Long Walk to Freedom
context: The personal price of leadership in the liberation struggle.
